title: tetramolopium crepatutarum
intro: Tetramolopium crepatutarum, also known as the Papua Wedelia, is a flowering plant species native to Papua New Guinea. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in local traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Tetramolopium crepatutarum typically involve the leaves and flowers being dried and made into teas or infusions for medicinal use.
